question: A 60-year-old postmenopausal woman with a history of heavy bleeding is considering hormone replacement therapy (HRT). Which option would be most appropriate for her?
explanation: A progestin intrauterine system (IUS) is the most appropriate choice for a postmenopausal woman with heavy bleeding due to perimenopausal ovulatory dysfunction. It provides contraception and reduces the risk of endometrial neoplasia.
reference: Women's Health Initiative Memory Study found an increased incidence of dementia in women older than 65 years initiating HT.
title: Management of Hormone Replacement Therapy in PostReproductiveAged Women
